Piranha 3D (2010)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $25,003,155Details
International Box Office $58,657,005
Worldwide Box Office $83,660,160
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,613,231
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,995,482
Total $10,608,713
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Every year the population of sleepy Lake Victoria explodes from 5,000 to 50,000 for Spring Break, a riot of sun and drunken fun. But this year, there's something more to worry about than hangovers and complaints from local old timers; a new type of terror is about to be cut loose on Lake Victoria. After a sudden underwater tremor sets free scores of the prehistoric man-eating fish, an unlikely group of strangers must band together to stop themselves from becoming fish food for the area's new razor-toothed residents.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$10,106,872 (40.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.47 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:29.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$24,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,470 opening theaters/2,491 max. theaters, 3.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $37,900,857

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 20th, 2010 (Wide) by Weinstein/Dimension
International Releases: May 17th, 2012 (Wide) (Lebanon)
Video Release: January 11th, 2011 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment, released as Piranha
April 17th, 2019 by Entertainment in Video
MPA Rating: R for sequences of strong bloody horror violence and gore, graphic nudity, sexual content, language and some drug use.
(Rating bulletin 2133, 8/11/2010)
Running Time: 88 minutes
Franchise: Piranha
Cast, crew, or production detail: Gratuitous Cameos, Remake
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion
Plot point: Animals Gone Bad
Social setting: Porn Business
Subgenre: Horror Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: IPW, Aja/Levasseur Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Every year the population of sleepy Lake Victoria explodes from 5,000 to 50,000 for Spring Break, a riot of sun and drunken fun. But this year, there's something more to worry about than hangovers and complaints from local old timers; a new type of terror is about to be cut loose on Lake Victoria. After a sudden underwater tremor sets free scores of the prehistoric man-eating fish, an unlikely group of strangers must band together to stop themselves from becoming fish food for the area's new razor-toothed residents.
